tweets.text: There is one fundamental thing that AI critics and “nitpickers” have never understood: AI capabilities advance & improve exponentially, now every few months, soon in weeks. Whatever they criticize today will soon be fixed. Haven’t they learned any lesson from the past 3 years?🧐

Pretty shocking result (that once again confirms what I wrote about the perils of distribution shift, 25 years ago):  

Translate coding benchmarks into languages LLMs can’t memorize and performance utterly falls apart.
“AI use impairs conceptual understanding, code reading, and debugging without delivering significant efficiency gains” — and that’s a quote from Anthropic’s own research!

Our new image generator MAI-Image-2 is out! Available now on MAI Playground for everything from lifelike realism to detailed infographics.

Our team has been pushing immensely hard for this release, and we are now among the top models out there: #3 family on @arena.

Check out https://t.co/2FkPUyrGYC
Welcome to OpenAI! Very excited to be working together and to make great tools to make developers everywhere more productive.

We are back to the phase of the AI news cycle where people are underestimating how jagged the AI ability frontier is, as well as how much they still depend on expert human decision-making or guidance at key points in order to function well. Still far from "doing all jobs," today.

And if you're the type of person who looks at Gauntlet AI and says, "I already make more than $200k/yr," note that's where the salaries _start_.

Our highest base salary ever is just shy of $1m/yr, but lots of folks make a lot more than $200k/yr.
Such an incredible moment to capture. 🤩
AI doesn't replace taste. It multiplies whatever taste you already have.

Google strikes back on vibe coding! The new @GoogleAIStudio updates look awesome! I'm particularly excited about the “Multiplayer+backend app support”, this will enable the design & development of incredible online worlds! Imagine if they also integrate their 3D world generator!
Great to see our new image model from our Superintelligence team rolling out in Copilot and coming soon to Foundry for enterprise customers.

Don’t use a calculator until you can do the math on your own.

Don’t vibe code until you can code – and debug and maintain code – on your own.

It’s that simple.

"What's the catch?" is probably the number 1 question we get with Gauntlet AI.

* It's very intense
* We're very selective
* We charge hiring partners recruiter fees

Gauntlet is all about finding + creating the most elite of AI engineers.

Companies happily pay for that.
Current AI is a librarian of existing knowledge. Science requires an explorer of the unknown. You don't win a Nobel Prize by staying in the library.
I’ve been saying it for a while, cursor’s research team is insanely high on talent density. So many people I respected from my PhD / early career ended up there. Seems like that’s bearing fruit.

For AI to stay responsive and economical at scale, it needs to move closer to where data is created and intelligence is used. 🌐

That’s where the AI grid comes in: connected, distributed sites operating as a unified, orchestrated computing platform.
